mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate compresses equity valuation multiples by raising the discount rate applied to future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST and RSAFS expansion signal fails to predict positive S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observed instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion signals strong manufacturing output, boosting industrial and materials sector earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with S&P 500 returns during confirmed manufacturing booms, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation exceeding the 2.5% threshold forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, increasing equity risk premiums and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when inflation crosses the 2.5% threshold, contradicting the risk premium compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth acceleration reduces recession probabilities, improving forward earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration consistently precedes negative S&P 500 returns over a 10-year backtest, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with elevated federal funds rates signals an overheating economy where monetary policy will aggressively curb demand, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS signal fails to predict negative S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observed instances during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Falling retail sales indicate weakening consumer spending, which directly impacts S&P 500 revenue streams and drives negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days during demand contraction phases.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not precede negative S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.
